mechanism_narrative: Sustained elevation of the federal funds rate above 1.5% signals monetary tightening that increases the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.13% as of 2018-02-15 indicates room for tightening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth decelerates below 100,000 net new jobs, signaling weakening labor demand that portends reduced consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 100,000 for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or correlation between PAYEMS growth and subsequent 60-day returns falls below 0.10 in absolute value over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) simultaneously fall below 700,000 annualized units, indicating joint contraction in consumer and residential demand that signals economic deceleration and negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month change negative AND HOUST < 700,000 units in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 406,748 million and HOUST at 746,000 units as of 2018-01-15
regime_hint: demand-side contraction with synchronized weakness across consumer and housing
failure_condition: When both RSAFS declines and HOUST < 700,000 occur jointly, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) decelerates with month-over-month growth falling below 0.1%, signaling supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization that constrains corporate profitability and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month percentage change < 0.1% for 1 month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO growth < 0.1%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce household income expectations and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months, or Spearman rank correlation between 3-month UNRATE change and subsequent 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter falls below 2.0% annualized rate, indicating subtrend economic expansion that reduces earnings growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice growth multiples downward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13564.5 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2018-10-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth < 2.0%, or correlation between GDPC1 growth rate and subsequent 60-day returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates with month-over-month growth exceeding 0.3%, combined with FEDFUNDS remaining below 1.0%, creating a regime where real rates are negative and monetary policy is accommodative despite inflation pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity investors benefit from negative real rates.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.3% AND FEDFUNDS < 1.0% in same month
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 230.102 and FEDFUNDS at 0.13% as of 2018-02-15
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: When both inflation accelerates > 0.3% and FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 650,000 annualized units while simultaneously retail sales (RSAFS) show negative year-over-year growth, indicating synchronized contraction in both residential and consumer demand that signals imminent economic slowdown and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 650,000 units AND RSAFS year-over-year growth < 0% in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 746,000 units and RSAFS at 406,748 million as of 2018-01-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction across housing and consumer sectors
failure_condition: When both HOUST < 650,000 and RSAFS year-over-year growth is negative, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year rolling window.
